canto_title: The Vision of the Celestial Lights and the Descent to Earth

scenes.summary: Beatrice reprimands Dante for his lack of awareness of his heavenly surroundings and instructs him to turn his gaze toward the other spirits.

scenes.scene_name: The Vision of the White Lights
scenes.summary: Dante turns back to see a multitude of brilliant, white, flower-like lights shining together.
Beatrice acknowledges Dante's presence but defers her full explanation to a more appropriate moment.
Beatrice identifies herself and describes the former glory of the monastery and its holy inhabitants.
Dante expresses his growing faith inspired by their warmth and asks to see Beatrice clearly.
Beatrice describes the ultimate celestial sphere where all desires are perfectly fulfilled.
Beatrice laments the physical and spiritual decay of the monastery and the decline of monastic rigor.
Beatrice reflects on how even the most promising beginnings can end in decline, using nature and scripture as examples.
The group of spirits gathers together and ascends rapidly like a whirlwind.
Beatrice signals Dante to follow, and he is pulled upward with a speed that surpasses his own power.
Dante recalls his profound emotional reaction to seeing the celestial sign of the Bull.
Dante offers a devotional prayer to the stars to gain the strength needed for his journey.
Beatrice tells Dante he is near salvation and commands him to look down at the world below.
Dante looks down through the spheres and views the Earth with a sense of disdain for its worldly appearance.
Dante observes the celestial bodies, including the sun, moon, and planets, and understands their relative positions and speeds.

scenes.scene_name: The Earth as a Small Garden
scenes.summary: Dante sees the entire Earth as a small, fertile garden stretching from mountains to the sea before turning back to Beatrice.
